name: Claude Code
analogy: Like having a senior developer sitting next to you — you describe what you want, it writes the code
whatItDoes: Claude Code is an AI-powered coding assistant made by Anthropic that runs in your terminal. You describe a feature, a bug fix, or a question in plain English and it reads your codebase, writes or edits the relevant files, and explains what it did. Unlike a simple autocomplete tool, it understands the full context of your project and can make coordinated changes across multiple files at once.
whyYouUseIt: As an HSE professional who did not come from a software engineering background, Claude Code made it possible to build SafetyIQ from scratch. When I needed to add a new feature — like filtering incidents by site or generating a PDF report — I described what I wanted and Claude Code implemented it, explained the approach, and helped me understand the code so I could maintain it myself.
whenToSwap: GitHub Copilot is better integrated into VS Code and works well for autocomplete as you type, if you prefer in-editor suggestions over a terminal-based workflow. Cursor is a full editor replacement that wraps VS Code with AI features built in. Codeium is a free alternative for basic autocomplete. Claude Code shines for large, multi-file tasks that require understanding the whole project.
